description_long: This crustacean features a robust, segmented exoskeleton with a rough, mottled brown and gray coloration, accented by subtle orange hues near the base of its limbs. Its body is divided into a broad, shield-like cephalothorax and a tapering, armored abdomen with distinct segmentations. The creature possesses six long, jointed legs that extend outward, giving it a wide stance and a spindly, spider-like appearance. The front of the body is equipped with two prominent, pointed appendages that resemble mandibles or mouthparts, but no large claws are visible. The surface texture appears rugged and matte, suggesting a hard, protective shell typical of terrestrial or aquatic crustaceans. No antennae or eyes are clearly visible, lending the creature a somewhat menacing and mysterious look.
description: A rugged, brown-gray segmented crustacean with six long, jointed legs and a hard, matte exoskeleton. It has a broad cephalothorax, a tapered abdomen, and prominent pointed mouthparts.
description_view_0: Front view shows the pointed mouthparts and the wide stance of the six legs, with the cephalothorax appearing broad and shield-like.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the length and articulation of the legs, the tapering abdomen, and the subtle orange coloration near the limb bases.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the segmented, tapering abdomen and the symmetrical arrangement of the legs extending outward.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizes the angular, jointed legs and the overall spiny, armored appearance of the body.
